name: Platform Staff Role Management
description: Grant/revoke/list Platform Administrator and Super Administrator roles for internal staff, backed by the user_roles table, replacing the single-owner-email/ADMIN_KEY-only path with real multi-user admin accounts.

frontend.evidence: frontend/admin-portal.html:365-369 (grantRole()) and its sibling revokeRole() call CDB_STAFF_AUTH.authFetch against /api/admin/roles/grant and /api/admin/roles/revoke; the page renders a grant/revoke form (:349-359) and a granted-roles table (:256-262), verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/admin-portal.html:272 self-declares 'Internal platform staff tool. Not indexed, not linked from any public page. Access requires a verified staff session.' Independently confirmed not linked from any customer-facing page — a repo-wide search for references to this page across frontend/*.html returns only staff-tool cross-links (frontend/proposal-generator.html, frontend/revenue-command-center.html, frontend/mssp-command-center.html). Non-discoverability from customer nav is by design, not a defect; see the accepted exception recorded in docs/capability-registry/exceptions.json.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/rbac.test.mjs:9-12 imports getPlatformRoles/isSuperAdmin/isPlatformAdmin/can/requireCan/handleGrantRole/handleRevokeRole/handleListRoles directly from workers/src/auth/rbac.js — confirmed by direct import-statement grep, 2026-07-08.

verification.evidence: workers/src/auth/rbac.js and frontend/admin-portal.html read directly; workers/src/index.js:8095-8105 grepped for route registration; workers/test/rbac.test.mjs import statements confirmed.

notes: This is a staff-only internal tool, not a customer-facing capability — customer_journey_complete and the GA vocabulary don't map cleanly onto it (there is no 'customer journey' to browser-verify). No actionable gap was found: backend, frontend, RBAC, and tests all check out for its actual (staff) audience. Priority P7 assigned by elimination (lowest-severity bucket) since none of P1-P6's conditions apply, not because documentation is thin.

name: Role/Plan-Based Frontend Feature Gating
description: Hide, disable, or otherwise adapt dashboard navigation and modules based on the authenticated customer's role and subscription plan, instead of rendering every module to every signed-in user regardless of entitlement.

frontend.evidence: PARTIALLY FIXED 2026-07-09. The sidebar's 17 nav-items still render unconditionally — investigation (backed by workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's PLAN_FEATURES/TIER_LIMITS, the authoritative per-tier entitlement source) found every tier including FREE has genuine, real, partial access to every one of the 17 pages (e.g. FREE gets 1 API key, not zero; FREE gets ai_analyze, not zero AI access) — so removing/hiding whole nav-items by tier would have been an unevidenced product guess, not a bug fix, and risks hiding functionality a customer genuinely has. Instead fixed the concrete, evidenced case where privilege-based feature availability was actually broken: exportCisoPDF(), syncPlanCards(), and selectPlan() compared the real, uppercase _plan.plan value ('FREE'|'STARTER'|'PRO'|'ENTERPRISE'|'MSSP') against lowercase literals, so their tier-gates silently never fired for any real account (e.g. a paying PRO customer's billing page never showed their plan as 'current', and clicking 'Upgrade to Pro' while already on Pro would re-initiate a new payment flow instead of short-circuiting). Fixed by normalizing the fetched value once per function rather than rewriting every comparison/DOM id. Separately found and fixed: TOOL_CATALOG/PLAN_QUOTA had zero MSSP entries despite MSSP being a real, top-tier backend plan (TIER_LIMITS.MSSP/PLAN_FEATURES.MSSP, both ENTERPRISE-or-better) — MSSP customers saw every tool locked and FREE-tier quota numbers on My Tools; loadKeys() read _plan?.plan?.key_limit (always undefined, since _plan.plan is a string not an object) instead of _plan?.key_limit, always showing a hardcoded '2'. A third case (initAiPage()/submitAiAnalysis()) LOOKED like the same case-sensitivity bug but was deliberately NOT 'fixed' the same way: workers/test/aiBrainEntitlementGate.test.mjs proves POST /api/ai/analyze is intentionally not plan-gated for any tier, so the stale FREE-tier block those functions contained was already-incorrect dead code the case bug was accidentally masking — correcting the case would have reintroduced a real regression (blocking FREE customers from a capability they're contractually, test-verified entitled to). That block and its adjacent fabricated 'queries left' credits counter (read a field, ai_queries_remaining, that GET /api/user/plan never returns) were removed instead.

navigation.evidence: The sidebar itself is the navigation; nav-items are not gated by tier/role (see notes for why), but the pages they lead to now correctly reflect each account's real entitlement where a real, evidenced gate exists.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userDashboardTierGating.test.mjs (2026-07-09, new) — 11 tests reading frontend/user-dashboard.html directly plus cross-checking workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's PLAN_FEATURES/TIER_LIMITS as the source of truth: confirms the AI-analysis FREE-tier block and its credits counter are gone, confirms exportCisoPDF()/syncPlanCards()/selectPlan() normalize case, confirms loadKeys() reads the real field, confirms every TOOL_CATALOG tiers[] array that includes ENTERPRISE also includes MSSP, confirms PLAN_QUOTA has an MSSP entry, and confirms MSSP's real backend entitlements match or exceed ENTERPRISE's on every PLAN_FEATURES flag. Independently re-verified live via a real headless-Chromium Playwright session (not just static grep): FREE tier confirmed unblocked on AI Analysis with zero JS errors; MSSP tier confirmed showing all 8 tools UNLOCKED and the real '9999/mo' quota (was: 0 tools, FREE quota); PRO tier confirmed showing their billing card correctly marked 'current' with a '✓ Current Plan' button (was: never, for any real account); a FREE-tier regression check confirmed exactly 1 tool (not all 8) shows unlocked, proving the fix didn't become over-permissive.

verification.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html and workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js read directly; workers/test/aiBrainEntitlementGate.test.mjs read to confirm the correct (not-gated) contract for /api/ai/analyze before touching that code path, specifically to avoid 'fixing' a case-sensitivity bug into a real regression. Full backend suite green: 182 files / 1927 tests (181/1916 baseline + 1 new file/11 tests). Live headless-Chromium Playwright session against the changed file confirms the fixed behavior across FREE/PRO/MSSP tiers plus a FREE-tier over-permissiveness regression check; zero uncaught JS exceptions.

notes: Re-scoped from the original finding after evidence-gathering: the original framing ('sidebar should hide items by tier/role') turned out to have no clean, safe implementation — workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's own PLAN_FEATURES/TIER_LIMITS show every tier has some real, legitimate access to every sidebar page, so wholesale hiding would have been an unauthorized product decision risking real customer-facing regressions (e.g. hiding 'API Keys' for FREE, who do have 1 real key). What genuinely WAS broken — and is now fixed — is that several already-written, already-intended tier-gates inside individual pages never actually fired due to a case-sensitivity bug (comparing the backend's real uppercase tier value against lowercase literals), which affected real paying customers (a PRO customer's own plan never showing as their 'current' plan; MSSP customers, the platform's top tier, seeing every tool locked). Remains P4/PILOT ONLY rather than closed: org-role-based gating (OWNER/ADMIN/ANALYST/MEMBER/VIEWER, see CAP-ORG-001) is still entirely unwired into this page — that's correctly out of scope until organization membership itself has a customer-facing UI, tracked separately.
